seoTitle: How to Connect PayPal to eBay for Buying (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to save your PayPal account as a payment method on eBay for faster, secure checkouts when buying items.
intro: While eBay runs its own managed payments system for sellers, you can still link a PayPal account for fast buyer checkouts. This guide shows how to save your PayPal wallet inside eBay's payment settings. It's for shoppers who prefer using PayPal at checkout rather than entering card details each time.

steps.heading: Open your account settings
steps.body: Log in to eBay, hover over your name in the top left, and click Account Settings from the dropdown.

steps.heading: Find payment options
steps.body: In the left sidebar under Payments, click Payment Options to view your saved methods.

steps.heading: Add PayPal as a payment method
steps.body: Click Add Payment Option, then select the PayPal radio button and continue.

steps.heading: Log in through the secure pop-up
steps.body: A PayPal login window will appear; enter your credentials and pass any two-factor verification.

steps.heading: Confirm and link
steps.body: Click Agree and Continue to bind your PayPal account to your eBay buyer profile, then verify it shows under Saved Methods.

faq.question: Can I use PayPal to receive payments as a seller on eBay?
faq.answer: Selling payouts on eBay typically run through eBay's own managed payments system rather than a linked PayPal account.

faq.question: Do I need to re-enter my PayPal login every purchase?
faq.answer: Once linked and saved, you generally won't need to re-enter credentials for every checkout unless your session expires.

faq.question: What if the PayPal pop-up doesn't appear?
faq.answer: Check that pop-ups aren't blocked in your browser settings, since the secure PayPal window needs to open to complete linking.

faq.question: Is there a fee for linking PayPal to eBay?
faq.answer: Linking itself is free; normal PayPal transaction fees may still apply depending on how you fund a purchase.
